docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Hello, my name is José San Martín Melio. I am a professional with over three years of experience in data analysis, gained at institutions such as the University of Santiago de Chile and the Council for Transparency. My work has focused on developing and analyzing information to support evidence-based decision-making and research.

My areas of expertise include transparency, corruption, ethics, environment and public policy evaluation. These interests have guided my career towards the design of strategies aimed at institutional and social improvement.

I have knowledge in R and Python programming languages, used to perform quantitative analysis, develop predictive models and produce data visualizations. I am currently pursuing a master's degree in Data Science, which allows me to strengthen my technical and methodological skills.

I live in Santiago de Chile and participate in interdisciplinary projects such as the UNESCO Chair on Transparency and Access to Information and FONDECYT 11230342.

I invite you to explore this space, where you will find detailed information about my projects, interests and work methodologies.

docs.title: Recommended bibliography
docs.text: The following is a bibliography on various topics that I have found particularly productive and/or stimulating.

docs.title: On society and social sciences
docs.text:
1. Luhmann N.(2007) La sociedad de la sociedad. México, Universidad Iberoamericana.
  2. Zürn, M. (2018). A theory of global governance: Authority, legitimacy, and contestation. Oxford University Press.
  3. Esposito, E. (2022). Artificial communication: How algorithms produce social intelligence. MIT Press.

docs.title: About statistics and data science
docs.text:
1. Stock, J. H., y Watson, M. W. (2020). Introduction to econometrics. Pearson.
  2. Hastie, T., Friedman, J. y Tibshirani, R. (2009). The elements of statistical learning: data mining, inference, and prediction. Springer
  3. Sarkar, D., Bali, R., y Sharma, T. (2018). Practical machine learning with Python. Springer

docs.title: Computational services
docs.text:
Using a workstation powered by a Ryzen 7 CPU, 32GB of RAM, and a graphics card with 16GB of VRAM, I offer services focused on:

  1. Tabulated transcriptions using OpenAI's whisper model —one of its few open-source services— enabling high-quality speech-to-text conversions.

  2. Utilization of open-source Large Language Models (LLMs) for generative tasks such as text summarization, translation, abstract concept identification, and more. Scalable models like Llama, DeepSeek, and [Mistral] (https://mistral.ai/en) are employed to ensure efficiency and adaptability.

  3. Use of fine-tuned BERT-based models for text classification tasks.

docs.title: CV
docs.text: I am a professional with 3 years of experience focused on social research and quantitative methodology, with a special interest in econometrics (causal inference) and machine learning (prediction).

docs.title: Professional experience
docs.text:
Currently - January 2024: Data analyst in the area of Studies. Transparency Council. In charge of conducting qualitative and quantitative studies on transparency, access to information, corruption and ethics.

Currently - January 2024: Assistant professor [UNESCO Transparency and Access to Information] (https://www.dep.usach.cl/es/catedra-unesco-transparencia-y-acceso-la-informacion-tyai). University of Santiago de Chile. Department of Political Studies.

December 2023 - March 2023: Advisor. Transparency Council. Advising on quantitative methodologies and the use of R language for the study of transparency.

February 2023 - November 2021: Freelance research assistant. Research Help Desk. Bibliographic management, use of Atlas.ti for bibliographic and qualitative analysis, design of quantitative research strategies through Stata, R and Excel.

Research assistant with Pamela Figueroa, Marcos Villalta, Nelson Paulus, Alexis Garrido. In areas related to: Party system, Electoral system, Transparency in the public sector, Ethics of social research, Research models in international studies, Crisis and political and social conflict.

December 2019 - April 2018 (non-degreed experience): Assistant in the area of Humanities and Economic Sciences in the institutional ethics committee of the University of Santiago de Chile performing work as a data entry clerk and reports through Excel and Stata platforms.

docs.title: Research experience
docs.text:
Currently - March 2023: Support staff. FONDECYT 11230342 “Cambio Político en Chile 1925-2022: ¿Institucionalismo Transformador?” funded by the Fondo Nacional de Desarrollo Científico y Tecnológico (FONDECYT) de Iniciación 2023 and led by Dr. Pamela Figueroa Rubio.

January 2020 - November 2019: Co-author of research with Alexis Garrido on ethics in CS "Ethical criteria to review research in Social Sciences. Systematization of an experience" led by Dr. Marcos Villalta.

docs.title: Teaching experience
docs.text:
July 2022 - April 2022: Teaching assistant to Professor Hugo Jofré. Quantitative Methods. University of Chile.

July 2022 - March 2022: Assistant Professor to Professor Hugo Jofré. Statistics. University of Santiago de Chile.

July 2022 - March 2022: Teaching assistant to Professor Camila Berríos. Subject of Political Science. University of Santiago de Chile.

January 2018 - August 2017: Teaching assistant to Professor Alfonso Dingemans. Asignatura Métodos Cuantitativos. Central University of Chile.

docs.title: My education
docs.text:
Postgraduate - Master (2024-2025): Student Master in Data Science. University of Santiago de Chile. Faculty of Administration and Economics.

Course evaluated +30hrs (2023): Certificate course on Quantitative and Spatial Data Analysis in R. University of Chile.

Undergraduate (2016-2021): Bachelor of Arts in International Studies. Degree in international affairs and policy analyst. University of Santiago de Chile. Department of Political Studies.
